contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC Asia Taichung Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Expected final ownership counts in a random-order divisor game.
English/Chinese statement mirror used to confirm the process and constraints.
No full public editorial was located during this pass; tags are based on the official tags plus a derivation from the random-order divisibility process.

annotation.summary: Compute each student's expected final number of cards when initially undealt cards are revealed in random order and won according to divisibility rules.
annotation.constraints: n <= 600, suggesting polynomial DP over cards/subsets of divisor relationships rather than simulation over all reveal orders.
annotation.core_idea: A revealed card can be won only by a currently owned multiple, and newly won cards can later serve as multiples for their divisors. The random order over undealt cards turns ownership propagation on the divisibility DAG into a probability DP: process dependency sets/combinatorial orders and add each card's ownership probability by linearity of expectation.
annotation.complexity: Polynomial in n with divisibility preprocessing; exact implementation depends on the chosen DP state.
tricks: random reveal order as topological probability
linearity of expectation over final owned cards

solution_variants.name: divisibility-random-order-dp
solution_variants.summary: Precompute divisor/multiple relations and use DP/combinatorial probabilities to assign ownership expectation.

tags.evidence: The official tag and constraints point to a polynomial state DP over the random divisibility process.
The expected values depend on counting relative reveal orders in the divisibility dependency graph.
Final expected counts can be decomposed into ownership probabilities for individual cards.
All transitions are determined by divisibility among card values.
